  "active_set": [
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which focus do you want for this section: (A) writing polish for clarity/conciseness without changing claims, (B) logic and evidence-claim checks to tighten arguments without style rewrites, (C) section/subsection reorganization and transitions while preserving content, or (D) positioning/novelty articulation versus nearest prior work?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "Check the internal argument chain across design, thresholding, and evaluation to ensure claims follow logically from premises.",
    "R1.3": "Verify that the reported metrics and ablations adequately support each stated claim in the text.",
    "R1.6": "Improve sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, conciseness, and flow without altering higher-level structure.",
    "RX.1": "Surface uncertainties and assumptions (e.g., generalization, calibration) to avoid presenting unwarranted certainty.",
    "RX.2": "Guard against overstating contributions or implications beyond what the reported evidence supports.",
    "RX.3": "Constrain assistance strictly to the provided section and the selected responsibilities.",
    "RX.4": "Avoid guidance that could lead to misleading routing claims or unsafe deployment inferences.",
    "RX.5": "Maintain clear linkage between claims and their evidence and document any proposed edits for traceability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which focus do you want for this section: (A) writing polish for clarity/conciseness without changing claims, (B) logic and evidence-claim checks to tighten arguments without style rewrites, (C) section/subsection reorganization and transitions while preserving content, or (D) positioning/novelty articulation versus nearest prior work?

rationale.R1.2: Check the internal argument chain across design, thresholding, and evaluation to ensure claims follow logically from premises.
rationale.R1.3: Verify that the reported metrics and ablations adequately support each stated claim in the text.
rationale.R1.6: Improve sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, conciseness, and flow without altering higher-level structure.
rationale.RX.1: Surface uncertainties and assumptions (e.g., generalization, calibration) to avoid presenting unwarranted certainty.
rationale.RX.2: Guard against overstating contributions or implications beyond what the reported evidence supports.
rationale.RX.3: Constrain assistance strictly to the provided section and the selected responsibilities.
rationale.RX.4: Avoid guidance that could lead to misleading routing claims or unsafe deployment inferences.
rationale.RX.5: Maintain clear linkage between claims and their evidence and document any proposed edits for traceability.
